title: cornus paniculata var. albida
intro: Cornus paniculata var. albida, also known as the White-stemmed Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its white stems and clusters of white flowers in late summer. The bark and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Cornus paniculata var. albida include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
